title: Notify LSP when files are deleted
description:
## Problem
LSP servers cache file contents and don't know when files are deleted. This causes stale references to be returned.

## Solution
When leta detects a file has been deleted (e.g., during format_locations), send textDocument/didClose notification to the LSP server to invalidate its cache.

Alternatively, implement workspace/didDeleteFiles notification if the LSP supports it.

## Related
This is a proper fix alongside leta-kay which is a workaround.
notes: Starting implementation: detect deleted files from open_documents cache
close_reason: Implemented cleanup_deleted_documents() that runs during ensure_document_open(). Sends textDocument/didClose for files that no longer exist on disk.

title: Optional variables in cctr: {{ var : optional str }} syntax
description:
## Use case

CLI tools sometimes show progress messages on stderr before actual output. These progress messages only appear when a command takes a while (e.g. first run with a cold cache). On subsequent runs, output is instant and no progress messages appear.

Need to write corpus tests that work both when progress messages appear AND when they don't.

## Proposed syntax

`{{ var : optional str }}` - matches zero or more lines of output. If the pattern matches, the variable is captured. If it doesn't match (the lines aren't there), the test still passes.

An alternative simpler approach: the optional variable matches the rest of the line it's on (and the line itself is optional), rather than spanning multiple lines.

## Example test

```
===
search for symbols
===
leta grep "User" -k class
---
{{ analyzing_text : optional str }}
src/models/user.py:15 [Class] User
src/handlers/auth.py:22 [Class] UserAuth
```

When progress appears, output would be:
```
Analyzing...
Analyzing... (42 symbols found)
src/models/user.py:15 [Class] User
src/handlers/auth.py:22 [Class] UserAuth
```

When there's no progress (cache hit), output would just be:
```
src/models/user.py:15 [Class] User
src/handlers/auth.py:22 [Class] UserAuth
```

Both should match the same test.

## Key idea

An `optional` type modifier on variables that makes the match non-required — the test passes whether or not those lines are present in the output.

title: Filter deleted files from refs results
description:
## Problem
leta refs returns references from files that no longer exist.

## Root cause
format_locations in mod.rs:184-213 checks file_path.exists() but only to skip reading context lines - the location is still included in results.

## Fix
Add a filter to skip locations where the file doesn't exist.

## Test
test/corpus/leta/cache_invalidation/refs_after_delete.txt
notes: Starting work on filtering deleted files
close_reason: Fixed by filtering deleted files in format_locations. Test passes.
